subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and explanations, and end with a conclusion.
Your child can read two-syllable words that have long vowel sounds and regular spelling.
Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to make their meaning clearer.
Your child can pick a photo or object that shows something they have done and tell one thing about it.
Your child can describe how a story is organized, including how the beginning starts the story and the ending wraps it up.
Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.
Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.
canonical_name: Grade 2 Language Arts: Reading and Writing — Foundational Skills and Text Comprehension
coherence_reason: The standards cover both reading foundational skills (phonics, word recognition), writing (informative texts, sentence construction, writing process), and text comprehension, which are distinct sub-concepts within language arts.

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: There is no grade 2 standard for this concept.
canonical_name: Grade 2 Language Arts: Not Applicable — No Standard for This Concept
coherence_reason: All standards indicate the absence or inapplicability of this concept for grade 2.
